buckets.description: Questions testing the fundamental properties of solids, liquids, and gases, such as having mass, occupying space, having a definite shape or volume, and compressibility.
Questions requiring the analysis of temperature-time graphs or tables of melting and boiling points to determine a substance's state at a given temperature or time.
Questions applying the concepts of evaporation, condensation, and freezing to explain everyday phenomena, particularly the formation of water droplets or ice on cold surfaces.
Questions that involve calculating or determining the volume of objects or substances, often using the water displacement method or by considering how matter occupies space in a container.
